raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Mega corporations are buying significant numbers of single-family homes, impacting homeownership opportunities for families.",
    "AB 2584 proposes to ban institutional investors that own more than 1,000 single-family homes from acquiring additional properties and converting them into rentals.",
    "As of June 2022, large institutional investors owned over half a million single-family homes in the U.S.",
    "Some firms own thousands of homes in California, specifically targeting areas with higher rent growth potential.",
    "Invitation Homes, affiliated with the private equity firm Blackstone, owns approximately 12,000 rental homes in California.",
    "Only 15% of California households could afford the median-priced home of $833,170 in late 2023.",
    "The homeownership rate in California has declined from about 60% in 2006 to roughly 55% in 2022.",
    "The decline in homeownership rates among Californians aged 35 to 45 was from about 49% in 2000 to nearly 40% in 2021.",
    "Corporate ownership of homes increased significantly after the Great Recession due to subprime mortgage foreclosures.",
    "Institutional investors can outbid working families for single-family homes by leveraging their wealth and purchasing properties in cash.",
    "Corporate investors tend to buy smaller, more modest homes that would typically be purchased by first-time homebuyers.",
    "Properties owned by Invitation Homes are concentrated in areas such as Sacramento, Stockton, and the San Fernando and San Gabriel valleys."
  ]
}
```
